svg.text: ESP32-S3-N16R8 | TB6600 | 2x TMC2209 | NEMA23 + 2x NEMA17 | 24V DC
NC (Normally Closed) | Active LOW | Internal Pull-up
Each switch: one terminal to GPIO pin,
other terminal to GND. NC = closed at rest.
Momentary NO | Active LOW | Internal Pull-up
Active LOW | Safety: only at HEATER station
1. TB6600 signal pins are differential: PUL+/DIR+/ENA+ to GPIOs, PUL-/DIR-/ENA- all to GND
2. TMC2209 VIO must connect to ESP32 3.3V (logic level reference)
3. EN/ENA is active LOW: LOW=enabled, HIGH=disabled (default in code)
4. STEP/PUL pulse: 5us HIGH per step, rising edge triggers
5. Limit switches: NC wired to GND, internal pull-ups on ESP32
6. Buttons: NO wired to GND, internal pull-ups on ESP32
7. Add 100uF electrolytic cap across VM/GND on each TMC2209
8. Heater relay: active LOW, only enabled at HEATER station (safety)
9. OLED is optional - system runs headless if not detected at 0x3C
10. Common ground is critical across all components
Total GPIO used: 18 of 27 available | 9 free for expansion
ESP32-S3 devboard center, oriented with USB at edge for programming
Group by harness for clean cable routing:
Harness 1: TB6600 signals (3 GPIO + GND) → screw terminal
Harness 2: TMC2209 Z-axis (3 GPIO + GND + 3.3V) → pin header
Harness 3: TMC2209 Rotation (3 GPIO + GND + 3.3V) → pin header
Harness 4: Limit switches (3 GPIO + GND) → JST connector
Harness 5: Buttons + Buzzer (3 GPIO + GND) → JST connector
24V input + 5V regulator on one edge, away from ESP32 logic
7 harnesses total | Use screw terminals for motor power, pin headers for signals
H1-H7 = Harness grouping for board layout
